description: 20-slide DALP deck on compliance controls and role-based governance

slides.title: DALP Compliance and RBAC
slides.subtitle: Policy-led controls for issuance, servicing, and redemption | March 2026

slides.title: Control Model
slides.subtitle: How DALP moves policy into execution

slides.title: Control is productized
slides.body:
• DALP positions compliance, identity, permissions, settlement coordination, and servicing as part of one operating model rather than bolt-on checks beside a token contract.
• The platform is built for regulated institutions that need governance, auditability, and production reliability across the full lifecycle.
• Compliance is enforced before execution, so blocked actions fail before the asset state changes on-chain.
• DALP sits between institutional systems and EVM-compatible networks, keeping the control plane explicit while integrating with existing core, custody, and risk environments.

slides.title: Control requirements mapped
table_data: Gate who may receive or transfer

slides.title: Templates structure controls
slides.body: Instrument templates and metadata schemas make product setup repeatable. DALP captures structured fields, policy choices, and required attributes inside governed design flows, so teams review the asset definition before launch instead of reconstructing it later from custom smart-contract changes.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/11 - Compliance and Policy Templates/Instrument Template - Qatar Real Estate - Metadata Schema.jpg

slides.title: Governance
slides.subtitle: Separation of duties in live operations

slides.title: Governance by separation
slides.item1:
Product setup
Define the asset structure, terms, and rule baseline in a governed workflow. The configuration stays reviewable before launch.
slides.item2:
Policy review
Compliance and legal teams inspect restrictions before the product goes live. That reduces rule drift across programs and jurisdictions.
slides.item3:
Approval gate
Maker-checker controls add explicit authorization before sensitive actions execute. Release responsibility is clear rather than implied.
slides.item4:
Execution rights
Permissions scope who may issue, transfer, service, pause, or redeem assets. Operational authority stays bounded by role.
slides.item5:
Emergency response
Native control options reduce exposure when an incident or exception appears. Teams can react without abandoning governance discipline.
slides.item6:
Evidence trail
Activity, monitoring, and audit records make responsibility visible after each step. Investigations do not depend on memory or email chains.

slides.title: Permissions stay explicit
slides.body: DALP exposes permissions during asset design so operational authority is visible before deployment. That supports cleaner separation between configuration, approval, and execution teams, and reduces the risk that governance logic lives only in off-platform procedures.
slides.image_path: templates/dalp-screenshots/03 - Asset Designer/Asset Designer - Step 7 - Asset Permissions Config.jpg

slides.title: Transfer decision flow
slides.body: This diagram illustrates the DALP compliance path around a transfer. The platform looks up the applicable token policy, checks identity and claims, evaluates transfer restrictions, and then either approves execution or blocks the movement before it settles. For institutional teams, the key point is ex-ante enforcement with an evidence trail after the decision.

slides.title: Lifecycle on one platform
slides.body: DALP is useful because compliance and governance do not stop at issuance. Asset design, identity onboarding, issuance, transfer, servicing, redemption, and reporting operate as connected stages. Metadata templates, claims, permissions, and ex-ante policy checks remain attached to the operating model instead of becoming manual controls once the asset is live.

slides.title: Lifecycle control points
slides.body: Each lifecycle step has a specific control objective. DALP matters when those objectives stay visible from setup through redemption, with operational outputs that product, compliance, and operations teams can evidence. The value is not only automation; it is a governed record of how the asset was designed, distributed, serviced, and closed out.

slides.title: Identity gates transfers
slides.body: DALP ties verified identity and claims to transaction permissions so wallet addresses alone are not the control model. That is central for regulated distribution, transfer restrictions, and reusable onboarding across asset programs.

slides.title: Auditability is operational
slides.body: Auditability in DALP spans more than transaction history. Operational activity, request logs, and monitoring views help teams investigate what was requested, what was executed, and which controls were involved. That makes governance usable for day-two operations, not just for architecture diagrams.

slides.title: Use Cases
slides.subtitle: How controls compose by asset class

slides.title: Regulated asset patterns
slides.col1:
Bonds
• Govern issuance with verified investor eligibility.
• Keep coupon and maturity events on-platform.
• Apply redemption logic with audit evidence.
• Reduce manual exception handling after launch.
slides.col2:
Funds
• Control who may subscribe, hold, and redeem.
• Reuse identity and compliance across distribution.
• Support servicing around fees and events.
• Keep day-two fund operations governed.
slides.col3:
Equity
• Enforce transfer restrictions before execution.
• Keep issuance and servicing on one control plane.
• Extend governance for regulated ownership models.
• Support auditable ownership change controls.

slides.title: Cash and alternatives
slides.col1:
Deposits
• Use governed issuance with permissioned operations.
• Fit bank-grade control boundaries and integrations.
• Keep evidence for live treasury workflows.
• Support repeatable approval-led operating models.
slides.col2:
Stablecoins
• Configure supply and transfer controls explicitly.
• Bind identity rules to distribution pathways.
• Operate on the same governed lifecycle model.
• Keep policy changes visible to operations teams.
slides.col3:
Real estate
• Combine ownership controls with verified identity.
• Apply approvals and transfer restrictions to live assets.
• Keep servicing and reporting audit-ready.
• Make registry-facing workflows easier to evidence.

slides.title: Why DALP for teams
slides.item1:
One control plane
DALP connects asset setup, compliance, identity, execution, servicing, and reporting instead of leaving teams to stitch them together after launch.
slides.item2:
Configurable governance
Templates, metadata schemas, permissions, and policy modules let teams adapt controls without rebuilding the operating stack for each program.
slides.item3:
Operational evidence
RBAC, maker-checker controls, activity logs, and monitoring make governance visible to product, operations, risk, and audit stakeholders.
slides.item4:
EVM deployment boundary
Approved wording is EVM-compatible network support. The deck does not imply DALP runs across non-EVM chains, and that boundary is explicit for sales and product discussions.
